titlestmt.author: compiled by Philip S. MacLeod and Christine Weideman

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday October 24, 2020 at 09:45

did.unittitle: Caribbean collection

descriptivenote.p: The collection is mainly in Spanish; Jamaica, Barbados, Trinidad, and Virgin Islands materials are in English.

did.abstract: A collection of correspondence; government documents, including legal cases, reports, decrees and proclamations; church documents; published illustrated materials; and writings from the Caribbean islands (Cuba, Jamaica, Santo Domingo, Barbados, and the Virgin Islands) on civil, military, religious, economic and social topics.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: The entire collection, except for Accession 2012-M-067, Accession 2013-M-021, and Accession 2017-M-014 is available on microfilm from Gale Group, Farmington Hills, Michigan.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Materials have been assembled from various collections and acquired from several individuals. They include materials transferred from the Latin American Collection Curator, Yale University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.The entire collection, except Accession 2012-M-067, Accession 2013-M-021, and Accession 2017-M-014 is available on microfilm. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown, though much of the material in this collection is likely in the public domain. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Caribbean Collection (MS 1780). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged by accession; the original accession is arranged by country, and by date within the country.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection includes correspondence; government documents, including legal cases, decrees, reports and proclamations; church documents; published illustrated materials; and writings from the Caribbean islands (Cuba, Jamaica, Santo Domingo, Barbados, and the Virgin Islands). The collection is arranged by accession; the original accession is arranged by country, and by date within the country. The description of each item includes, when possible, its author, title, place of origin, and a summary of its contents. When an item is a copy of an original, it is arranged by the date of the original. The date field, however, contains the date of the copy itself, not the original. The date of the original is included in the summary description of the item.

did.unittitle: Original accession
Cuba

Author: Villalobos, Gabriel Fernandez de (Marquis de Varinas). Title: Grandeza de Yndias Estado eclesíastico, político y civil de ellas. Location: [Havana?] Description: Handwritten copy of a memorial [directed to the king of Spain?] which focuses on the economic, geographical, and military circumstances of the provinces of the Spanish empire. The major focus of the work is the Caribbean Basin region and the ports of that area. Also included is a discussion of the general political and economic problems facing the empire. This copy was made in 1849 in Madrid by Domingo Guzman del Monte from a copy. Based on the 1990 published edition of the work, Guzman del Monte's copy may be one of only two in existence.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: "Carta que escrivío el Rey Nuestro Señor Don Felipe Quinto al señor príncipe su hijo." Location: San Yldefenso. Description: Copy of the letter by which Felipe V abdicated the Spanish throne in favor of his son, Luis. A note at the end of the letter says Friar Agustin de Mesnones, Padre Comisario de San Francisco, died in Havana.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: "Hace presente a V. M. su dictamen sobre el estado actual y negocios pendientes de la Real Compañía de la Havana." Location: Madrid. Description: A review, by the new presiding officer of the Havana monopoly trading company, of the history, finances, and present state of the company, for the king of Spain. Also included are recommendations for actions that should be taken.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Espolio del reverendo obispo defunto. Description: Copy of a case involving property/money belonging to Santiago Echavarria, the deceased bishop of Havana. The names of Luis Peñalver and Manuel Casimiro de Arango, who held the bishop's power of attorney, appear throughout the document. The case also involves the audiencia of Mexico and a royal cedula which deals with the establishment of a "Colegio de Misionarios" of the Capuchin Order and the "oratorio" of Saint Philip of Neri in Havana.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Pedro Xavier de Vera Correspondence. Locations: Havana, Santo Domingo, Puerto Rico, Puerto Príncipe Isla de Cuba, Ciudad Real, Tuxtla, Guatemala, Sololá, Cádiz, Madrid, Cuenca, Quito, Lima, Popayán, Mompox, Santa Fe de Bogotá, Cartagena, Turbaco, Santa Marta, Mérida. Description: Personal and professional correspondence (incoming and outgoing and including correspondence of others) of a business agent, who was a resident of the royal court in Madrid. Also included are legal documents, such as grants of powers of attorney, statements of accounts, and receipts of payments. Many of the materials originated in the islands of the West Indies; they are arranged chronologically by individual.
Dominguez, Bernardo

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Marquez, Manuel

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Garcia Ballecillos, Gabriel

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Robles, Mariano

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Leon y Acero, Antonio de

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Goyena, Joaquin

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Good conduct certificate. Description: Handwritten English translation stating that Juan [Nepomureno?] de Atrocha discharged the duties of the lord secretary of the General Administration of Privileges, with approbation, during the latter's illness.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Author: Manzano, Juan Francisco. Title: "Obras completas de Juan Francisco Manzano esclavo de la Isla de Cuba." Description: Handwritten bound copy of the writings of a freed Cuban slave. The volume includes Manzano's autobiography, letters, poetry and the drama, Zafira. Also included is a list of people who contributed money to purchase Manzano's freedom. An inscription on a front page reads: "To the Academy of Studies; in its lack or due to its destruction, to its founder and my friend Don Francisco Feyer y Diago. - Madrid- 15 January 1852 Nicolas M. de Azcarate."

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: The Francisco Vives Papers. Description: Official correspondence and other documents related to attempts to resupply and aid an abortive invasion of Mexico by Spanish troops. Also discussed is the later effort to dispatch ships to pick up the troops of the defeated Spanish army. Some correspondence is written on the official stationery of the "Captaincy General of the always faithful island of Cuba."

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Albion W. Knight correspondence. Place: Havana; Stockbridge, Massachusetts; Brunswick, Maine; and New York. Description: Series of twenty two letters written by Albion W. Knight (1859-1936), an Episcopal bishop of Cuba, to Frances de Salles Carroll. The letters deal with personal and religious matters.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Jamaica
Title: Will. Description: Copy of the last will and testament of David Dehany.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Lease. Description: Lease of the Barbican and Point Estates and a cattle property, by Philip Dehany to John Tomlinson. Text contains a list of the names of the slaves on each plantation.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Map. Description: Handdrawn map of the Point Estate owned by Philip Dehany.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Map. Description: Handdrawn map of the Barbican Estate owned by Philip Dehany.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Andrew F. Evans papers. Description: Series of four documents related to a naval battle near Tortugas between the British ship [ Aolus ?], under the command of Andrew Evans, and the Spanish ship [ Urquiss ?]. Included are a list of damages to the [ Urquiss ?], a summary of casualties, and two reports about the incident.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Conveyance. Description: "Conveyance of certain plantations in the Island of Jamaica and declaration of the trust thereof and of certain other securities within mentioned," made by Leonard Parkinson and others to Martin Williams and others.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Release. Description: "A release of estates in Tortola Pt. in confirmation of his marriage articles." This release was made by William Payne Georges to John Broadhead and others.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Statement of division. Description: Document, possibly related to the estate of Jemima Cussans, discussing the division of the Amity Hall Estate slaves among Cussans and other parties.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Will and instructions. Description: A copy of the last will and testament of Mary Salter Dehany. Also included is a written instruction to lawyers to prepare a conveyance transferring Point, Paradise, and Barbican Estates, to William Knight Dehany.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Draft power of attorney. Description: Draft of a joint power of attorney made by William Knight Dehany and Barbara Wilhelmina Traill to George Atkinson.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Conveyance and assignment. Description: Draft by which Barbara Wilhelmina Traill conveys and assigns the Point, Paradise, and Barbican Estates, as well as Kempshot Penn, to William Knight Dehany.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: List of charges. Description: "Charges of obtaining exemplification of the probate of will and codicil of Jemima Cussans, deceased spinster."

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Power of attorney. Description: "Draft power of attorney to prove will of Jemima Cussans and to act generally in Jamaica." Act made by William Knight Dehany and Matthew Scott, executors and trustees under the will of Jemima Cussans, to George Atkinson, James Hozier, and John Gale.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Counterclaim. Description: Counterclaim by William Knight Dehany and Matthew Scott, executors of, and trustees for, Jemima Cussans, against John Nelson Bond, claiming they were entitled to one quarter of the value of the slaves of the Amity Hall Estate, which were held by Bond. Included is a document listing the number of Amity Hall Estate slaves by category of work performed and the total estimated value of each group.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Correspondence. Description: Brief letter by Charles Scott, former attorney for Amity Hill Estate, addressed to an unknown party, concerning past registrations of the slaves of Amity Hill Estate.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Newspaper clipping. Description: Article from an unidentified newspaper entitled "Compensation to the West India proprietors," concerning the abolition of slavery in Britain.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Correspondence. Description: Brief letter by Mick Clayton to [William Knight Dehany?] concerning his counterclaim to a portion of the value of slaves held by John Nelson Bond.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Conveyance. Description: Conveyance of lands on Annatto Bay, named Prospect Place, by John MacPherson MacNeal to Alexander Stewart.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Conveyance. Description: "Conveyance to the uses of a moiety of a wharf and premises in Kingston, Jamaica." Act made by Mr. and Mrs. James Simpson and others to Donald Campbell.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Supplemental abstract. Description: "Supplemental abstract of title to a moiety of a wharf and premises in Kingston, Jamaica." [Document is related to the probate of the estate of James Carson.]

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Conveyance. Description: "Conveyance of the equity of redemption in a plantation called Gilbralter, and other estates and premises in the Island of Jamaica," and other matters, by Roger Ryanston and others to Alexander Stewart and trustees.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Conveyance. Description: Conveyance of an unidentified property under decree by Alexander Stewart and others to James Towson.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Release. Description: "Release of the estates of Ralph Parkinson and Matthew Parkinson from 1/19 part of a legacy of £1000," on [Whitehall Plantation?] by Mr. and Mrs. Hugh Woodmass to Mr. and Mrs. J. A. G. Clarke.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Power of attorney. Description: Draft power of attorney from William Knight Dehany to William Hutton to manage Points Estate.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Power of attorney. Description: Draft power of attorney by William Knight Dehany to Richard Francis David and Neil Malcolm to manage Points Estate.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Release, copy and conveyance. Description: Release of interests in real estates; certified copy of an indenture; and "conveyance of 1/3 of a wharf and premises in Kingston, 1/3 of Constant Spring and Snow Hill Plantations, and 1/3 of ½ of Haming Plantation," by William Christie to James Porteous and others.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Release. Description: "Release of the equity of redemption of plantations in the Island of Jamaica," by James Graham Clarke and others to Edward Chard.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Conveyance. Description: Conveyance and assignment of trust estates and interests in Gibralter and [Paradise?] Plantations and other places.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Mortgage abstract. Description: Abstract of mortgage of the Mears Plantation in the Island of Jamaica, and assignment of policies of insurance, and a declaration of trust of the mortgage debt," from John Mitchell to Roland Mitchell and Francis Henry Mitchell.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Grant. Description: "Grant and limitation of a moiety of [Wheelerfield?] Plantation in the Island of Jamaica to uses to secure an annual sum or yearly rent charge to the said Frederick Milner." Act made by Thomas Henry Milner.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Conveyance. Description: "Conveyance and assignment of Albion Estate in the Parish of St. David in the Island of Jamaica with the cattle, stock, and effects thereon." Act made by Thomas and Caroline Henrietta Hibbert to James Porteous and James Carson. A map of the property is included.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Confirmation. Description: William Morgan attests to the number of pages in the Albion Estate conveyance documents.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Docket of conveyance. Description: Docket of conveyance and assignment of the Green Valley coffee plantation in Port Royal and St. David Parishes in the county of Surrey by James Porteous and others to Donald Campbell.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Correspondence. Description: Brief letter by Alexander Stewart to W. S. Cookson, concerning the Gibralter and Charlottenburgh Plantation conveyances.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Conveyance. Description: Draft conveyance of Prospect Penn, and Gibralter, May Hill, Camberwell and Charlottenburgh Plantations, by Alexander Stewart, Sr. and Alexander Steward, Jr. to Henry Westmoreland.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Correspondence. Description: Brief letter by Alexander Stewart to W. S. Cookson concerning the Gibralter and Charlottenburgh Plantation conveyances.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Conveyance. Description: Conveyance of a plantation and estate in the parish of Vere, by Colonel Edward Goulborn to James William Mitchell.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: [Payment schedule?] Description: Schedule of payments apparently related to the conveyance of the Gibralter and Charlottenburgh Plantations to Henry Westmoreland.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Title abstract. Description: Abstract of title to certain sugar and coffee plantations named Constant Spring, Snow Rose, [Haming?], Cedar Valley, The Spring, Shortwood, and Hermitage. Related to the estate of James Carson.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Correspondence. Description: Brief letter concerning a mortgage by W. Strickland to James William Mitchell.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Mortgage. Description: "Mortgage of the absolute reversion expectant upon the decease of Mrs. James Mitchell to the residue of £19,321/16/6 reduced annuities after raising thereout £12,000 sterling with interest at five per cent from her death and of Mr. Mitchell's interest in the trust funds comprised in his marriage settlement to secure £2000 with interest at six per cent reducible to five per cent on punctual payment." Mortgage made by James William Mitchell to William Strickland Cookson. A reconveyance dated 1884 is attached.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Correspondence. Description: Brief letter by [Graham?] addressed to Clayton and Cookson concerning a [deed?].

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Abstract. Description: "Supplemental abstract of title to the Northbrook Estate in the Parish of St. Andrew." [Related to the James Carlson estate.]

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Conveyance. Description: "Conveyance of five pieces of land, part of the Monymusk Estate in the Parish of Vere Jamaica," by John Morant to James William Mitchell.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Conveyance. Description: "Conveyance of Shortwood and Hermitage Plantations" by Mrs. Sarah Gordon to James Carson." Included are maps of the two properties.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Conveyance. Description: "Conveyance of the plantation known as Temple Hill with land and hereditaments in St. Andrews, Jamaica" by Mrs. C. S. Chrystie to James Carson. Included is a map of the property.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Abstract. Description: "Supplemental abstract of title to a plantation known as Temple Hill with land and heredits in the Island of Jamaica."

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Marriage contract. Description: "Articles on the marriage of Marriott S. Carson and Florence M. V. Cox." Also contains materials related to the Cold Spring Estate and the death of James Carson.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Conveyance. Description: "Conveyance of one moiety of premises in Port Royal Street in Kingston, Jamaica," by Francis Porteous and others to James Carson.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Abstract. Description: "Supplemental abstract of the title of James Simpson Carson to a moiety of a wharf and premises at Kingston, Jamaica."

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Mortgage. Description: "Mortgage of the Albion Estate or sugar plantation in the Parish of S. Thomas Parish in the Island of Jamaica by way of collateral security for the sum of £6100 and interest at six per cent per annum, reducible to five per cent," by Mrs. Sarah Marriott Carson and Major James Carson to R. A.Wainewright. A reconveyance dated 1884 is attached to the mortgage.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Schedule. Description: "Re: Carson deceased, schedule of deeds and documents relating to Cold Spring and other estates, Jamaica and receipt of Misters Cookson, Wainwright, and Pennington for the same."

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Release. Description: Release by Margaret Rennalls to Charles [Edmond?] Gunter. An earlier draft is also included.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: The Anti-Slavery Reporter and Aborigines' Friend . Description: Copy of the April 1924 edition of the anti-slavery journal published in London.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Will. Description: Extract from the last will and testament of Thomas Cussans.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Letter. Description: A letter from Cookson to J. Mitchell concerning a deed.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: William Knight Dehany conveyance and correspondence. Description: A conveyance to William Knight Dehany; a note concerning a grant of power of attorney by Dehany; and a letter from Dehany to [Clayton?].

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Santo Domingo

Title: "De algunas notables cosas que hay anacahesido en diversas partes de las Yndias del mar océano." Description: Brief handwritten volume of passages, probably drawn from an unidentified text, concerning geographical oddities and strange occurrences in Hispanola, Peru, Nicaragua and Panama. The volume contains a page by page English translation.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Title: "Por Don Rodigo Pimental clérigo de menores ordenes" Description: Legal case in which Pimental claimed Salvador de Caseda and Christobal Martinez impugned his honor by giving false and contradictory testimony in a criminal case involving accusations against him. The text includes detailed arguments on four articles used to prove Pimental's case.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: Royal order concerning Juan Francisco Mesines. Place: Buen Retiro. Description: Ruling by the king of Spain on a claim of immunity presented by religious authorities in a civil case.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: "Relación de los meritos y servicios del Bachiller don Perdo Valera...." Place: Madrid. Description: Published document on the career of Pedro Valera, a priest who served in the Iglesia Metropolitana de Santo Domingo.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Author: Leveaux, Esteban. Title: Proclamation. Place: Guarico (Cap-Haitien). Description: Proclamation by the governor of Saint Domingue that Spain ceded control of the entire island of Hispanola to France under the Treaty of Basel. The document also guarantees the Spaniards their property rights, praises the French Republic, and criticizes the Spanish monarchy.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Santo Domingo/Puerto Rico

Title: Royal decrees concerning Santo Domingo and Puerto Rico. Location: [Madrid?], Aranjuez. Description: Series of royal decrees and orders addressed to the governor of Puerto Rico or the audiencia in Santo Domingo, dealing with trade and the seizure of foreign shipping. Also included are decrees and orders dealing with general fiscal matters.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Virgin Islands

Title: Reports by the governor of the Virgin Islands. Description: A series of twelve typewritten reports (ten are annual reports) written by the governor of the Virgin Islands to the United States secretary of the navy or the United States president. The reports deal with such matters as finances, public health and welfare, police affairs, government structure, and education.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Title: "Review of Work Relief Activities in the Virgin Islands of the United States." Description: Report concerning public relief projects carried out in the Virgin Islands between April 1, 1934 and June 30, 1935.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 253 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Additional material

Title: Records of the sale of Coopers Hill Plantation in Barbados by Samuel Estwick of London to a group of three investors: Edward Lovedon, John Tritton and Bertie Willoughby. Description: English manuscript indenture on nine vellum leaves, approximately 24x27 inches each. As part of the assets of the plantation the document records 104 slaves, listed by name within subdivisions of men (32), women (35), boys (14) and girls (23).
Page 1
Pages 2-9, folded at the bottom and joined by ribbon and red resin seals
Additional material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession consists of published illustrated materials of scenes and events in Cuba, Haiti, and Trinidad.

did.unittitle: Cuba
Title: "Pacification with the Maroon Negroes." Illustrator: Augustin Brunias. Publisher: Scott B. Edwards.
Title: "Trelawney Town, the Chief Residence of the Maroons." Illustrator: Unknown. Publisher: Unknown
Title: "Ritratto di Touissant Louverture." Illustrator: Unknown. Publication: Teatro Universale , page 1.
Title: "Town and Harbour of Havanah." Illustrator: Unknown. "Publication: The Illustrated London News , page 320.
Title: "St. Jago De Cuba, Sketched from the Harbour." Illustrator: Unknown. Publication: The Illustrated London News , page 325.

Title: Havannah--Part of the Harbour--Fort of Atares, Where the 50 Invaders Were Shot," "Entrance to Havannah from the Fuerte Del Principe." Illustrators: Unknown. Publication: The Illustrated London News , page 444.
Title: "Explosion of the Powder Magazine, in the Arsenal, Havana," "The Roadstead and City of Havana." Illustrators: Unknown. Publication: Illustrated Times , page 356.
Title: "Hackney Carriage, Havana," "A Balcony at Havana," "The Place D'Armes, Havana." Illustrators: Henri Valentin, Unknown. Publication: Illustrated Times , page 45.
Title: "View of Havanna from a Drawing by Captain Montagu O'Reilly, R.N." Illustrator: Smyth. Publication: The Illustrated London News , page 280.
Title: "The Place of Arms at Havannah, Cuba." Illustrator: Unknown. Publication: The Illustrated London News , page 193.
Title: "Circa De Las Posas, on the Pan De Guajaybon, Cuba." Illustrator: Unknown. Publication: The Illustrated London News , page 393.
Title: "L'Ile De Cuba--Vue de Malanzas." Illustrator: Unknown. Publication: L'Illustration, Journal Universel , page 273.
Title: "L'Ile De Cuba." Illustrator: Unknown. Publication: L'Illustration, Journal Universel , pages 260-261.
Title: "The Capture of the Virginius--Sketches at Santiago Del Cuba." Illustrator: Unknown. Publication: The Graphic , page 541.
Title: "Views in Santiago De Cuba." Illustrator: Unknown. Publication: The Graphic .
Title: "Tree and Chapel of Christopher Columbus, Havana," "The Manufacture of Sugar--Cutting the Cane." Illustrators: Unknown. Publication: The Graphic , page 33.
Title: "The Horrors of the War: Feeding Starving Refugees from Santiago at Caney." Illustrator: C.H.S. Publication: The Graphic , page 220.
Description: A portrait of children sitting on a rock outcropping with the skyline and harbor of Havana in the background. Illustrator: Unknown. Publication: Unknown

Title: "La Mort De Maceo. Les Insurgés Cubains Disputant Le Corps de Leur Général Aux Soldats Espagnols." Illustrator: Unknown. Publication: Supplément Littéraire Illustré du Petit Parisien , page 416
Title: "The Spanish-American Crisis: Views of Havana, The Capital of Cuba." Illustrator: G. Montbard. Publication: The Illustrated London News , page 574.
Title: "The Spanish-American War: Views on the Coast of Cuba." Illustrator: Holland Trigham. Publication: The Illustrated London News , page 742.

Title: "Entrance to the Harbour of Santiago De Cuba; With the Merrimac , which was Sunk at the Narrowest Portion of the Channel." Illustrator: G. Montbard. Publication: The Illustrated London News , page 1.
Title: "The Spanish-American War: The Americans Storming the Hill of San Juan." Illustrators: W. Hatherell, J. Gulich. Publication: The Graphic , pages 196-197.
Title: "The Surrender of Santiago: Hoisting the United States Flag on the Governor's Palace." Illustrator: John Charlton. Publication: The Graphic , pages 264-265.
Title: "Panoramic View of the Great Battle of the Spanish-American War: The General Assault on Santiago De Cuba on July 1." Illustrator: Unknown. Publication: The Graphic .
Haiti
Title: "Les Evénements De Haiti." Illustrator: Unknown. Publication: Supplément Illustré du Petit Journal, page 408.
Trinidad
Title: "Carnival in Port of Spain, Trinidad." Illustrator: Unknown. Publication: The Illustrated London News , pages 496-497.
Additional material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession includes assorted printed materials from Cuba and Jamaica dating from the 19th and early 20th century, including a report that concerns the slave trade.

did.unittitle: Cuba
Reglamento del Monte de Piedad de la Habana
Reglamento sobre establecimientos insalubres, peligrosos e incomodos
Reglamento para los arquitectos municipales de la Habana
Reglamento de las compañias sanitarias
Servicio de higiene especial : reglamento general para la Isla de Cuba
Legal documents concerning bankruptcy of Havana company, Canes Fernandez
Jamaica
Quarterly Reports on Horned Stock Slaves at the St. Helen's Cattle Pen, Jamaica
